source.title: NADPH biosensor-based identification of an alcohol dehydrogenase variant with improved catalytic properties caused by a single charge reversal at the protein surface

initial_request.research_problem: Improve catalytic properties of LbADH for 2,5-hexanedione reduction using NADPH biosensor-based FACS screening to overcome throughput limits.
initial_request.known_context: LbADH catalyzes NADPH-dependent reduction of prochiral ketones. Conventional screening methods are low-throughput, limiting directed evolution efforts.
constraints: Must use NADPH biosensor pSenSox for fluorescence-based detection
Screening must be performed in E. coli
quantifiable_goals: Identify LbADH variants with improved activity for 2,5-hexanedione reduction
Achieve higher fluorescence signal in biosensor assay compared to wild-type

evolution_trajectory.state_before: No high-throughput screening method available for LbADH variants.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How to screen large LbADH libraries for improved 2,5-hexanedione reduction activity?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: A multi-step FACS strategy using the pSenSox NADPH biosensor can enrich cells expressing LbADH variants with higher activity.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Design a FACS screening strategy with three positive selection steps followed by one negative selection step.
evolution_trajectory.observation: The entire screening process comprised three positive selection steps for cells showing high fluorescence after biotransformation of 2,5-hexanedione followed by a negative selection step. In the latter, cells showing high fluorescence independent of 2,5-hexanedione were excluded by collecting only those cells in the library that showed the same fluorescence as E. coli TOP10/pSenNeg cells lacking an active LbADH.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: The designed strategy needs to be implemented by generating a mutant library.

evidence.quote_or_span: The entire screening process comprised three positive selection steps for cells showing high fluorescence after biotransformation of 2,5-hexanedione followed by a negative selection step.

In the latter, cells showing high fluorescence independent of 2,5-hexanedione were excluded by collecting only those cells in the library that showed the same fluorescence as E. coli TOP10/pSenNeg cells lacking an active LbADH.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: FACS screening strategy designed but no mutant library available.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Need to generate a diverse library of LbADH variants for screening.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Error-prone PCR can introduce random mutations across the LbADH gene to create a library of ~4×10^6 clones.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Generate random mutagenesis library of LbADH by error-prone PCR.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 4 × 10^6 clones created by random mutagenesis we identified the variant LbADH^{K71E}.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Library is ready for FACS screening.

evidence.quote_or_span: 4 \times 10^{6}$ clones created by random mutagenesis we identified the variant $LbADH^{K71E}$ .

evolution_trajectory.state_before: LbADH mutant library generated but not yet screened.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Which clones in the library have improved activity for 2,5-hexanedione reduction?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: FACS can sort cells with high fluorescence after biotransformation, enriching for improved variants.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform FACS screening: analyze 2.5×10^5 cells, sort 240 high-fluorescence cells, recover colonies.
evolution_trajectory.observation: 240 library cells showing a higher fluorescence than the reference were spotted on LB agar plates; 83 out of the 240 spotted cells formed colonies (35% recovery).
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Recovered clones need to be validated for fluorescence phenotype.

evidence.quote_or_span: 240 library cells showing a higher fluorescence than the reference were spotted on LB agar plates
83 out of the 240 spotted cells formed colonies
83 cells (35%) formed colonies and were further analyzed.
The entire screening process comprised three positive selection steps for cells showing high fluorescence after biotransformation of 2,5-hexanedione followed by a negative selection step.

The strategy used to isolate cells containing LbADH variants with improved activity for reduction of 2,5-hexanedione included three enrichments steps with positive selection followed by one negative selection step and another positive selection step.

Several of the clones showed an increased specific fluorescence in the presence of 2,5-hexanedione compared to the absence of 2,5-hexanedione and a higher specific fluorescence than the reference strain with wild-type LbADH.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: 83 colonies recovered from FACS but their fluorescence phenotype unconfirmed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Do the sorted clones truly exhibit increased specific fluorescence compared to wild-type?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: BioLector cultivation will confirm increased specific fluorescence for several clones.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Cultivate clones in BioLector system with and without 2,5-hexanedione and measure specific fluorescence.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Several of the clones showed an increased specific fluorescence in the presence of 2,5-hexanedione compared to the absence of 2,5-hexanedione and a higher specific fluorescence than the reference strain with wild-type LbADH.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Promising clones should be sequenced to identify mutations.

evidence.quote_or_span: Several of the clones showed an increased specific fluorescence in the presence of 2,5-hexanedione compared to the absence of 2,5-hexanedione and a higher specific fluorescence than the reference strain with wild-type LbADH.
83 cells (35%) formed colonies and were further analyzed.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Several clones confirmed with increased fluorescence, but mutations unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: What mutations are responsible for the improved phenotype?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Sequencing will reveal the genetic changes in the improved clones.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Sequence plasmids from four clones with highest fluorescence.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Sequencing of the plasmids isolated from four of these clones revealed that all carried a single G→A transition in the Lbadh gene resulting in the amino acid exchange K71E.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Need to confirm that K71E alone causes the phenotype, not other mutations.

evidence.quote_or_span: Sequencing of the plasmids isolated from four of these clones revealed that all carried a single G→A transition in the Lbadh gene resulting in the amino acid exchange K71E.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: K71E mutation identified but causality not confirmed.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Is the K71E mutation alone sufficient for the improved fluorescence?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Re-cloning K71E into fresh pSenSox will confirm that the mutation alone causes increased fluorescence.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Amplify Lbadh^{K71E} gene by PCR, replace wild-type Lbadh in pSenSox, and test in BioLector.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Cultivation of the recombinant strain carrying pSenSox-Lbadh^{K71E} in a BioLector system confirmed that it shows a higher specific fluorescence in the presence of 2,5-hexanedione than in the absence, and higher fluorescence than E. coli with wild-type LbADH. This result supported the assumption that the K71E mutation in LbADH was responsible for the increased fluorescence and leads to improved properties for 2,5-hexanedione reduction.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Confirmed variant should be purified for detailed characterization.

evidence.quote_or_span: This result supported the assumption that the K71E mutation in LbADH was responsible for the increased fluorescence and leads to improved properties for 2,5-hexanedione reduction.

To confirm that this mutation, rather than secondary mutations in the pSenSox plasmid or in the genomic DNA, was responsible for the increased fluorescence during the biotransformation of 2,5-hexanedione, the $Lbadh^{K71E}$ gene was amplified by PCR, used to replace the wild-type Lbadh gene in plasmid pSenSox, and transferred into E.

Cultivation of the recombinant strain carrying pSenSox- $Lbadh^{K71E}$ in a BioLector system confirmed that it shows a higher specific fluorescence in the presence of 2,5-hexanedione than in the absence, and higher fluorescence than E.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: K71E variant confirmed but not biophysically characterized.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Does the K71E mutation affect protein structure or oligomeric state?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Purification and SEC/SDS-PAGE/DLS will reveal any structural changes.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Purify LbADH^{WT} and LbADH^{K71E} with N-terminal StrepTag-II and analyze by SEC, SDS-PAGE, and DLS.
evolution_trajectory.observation: LbADH^{WT} eluted with an apparent molecular mass of 95 kDa and LbADH^{K71E} with an apparent molecular mass of 113 kDa. SDS-PAGE also suggested a shift. DLS showed no significant difference in hydrodynamic radius, but the consistent elution volume difference indicated a structural change triggered by the K71E exchange.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Stability of the variant should be assessed.

evidence.quote_or_span: To characterize and compare the $LbADH^{K71E}$ variant with the $LbADH^{WT}$ , both enzymes containing an N-terminal StrepTag-II were overproduced in E.
$LbADH^{WT}$ eluted with an apparent molecular mass of 95 kDa and $LbADH^{K71E}$ with an apparent molecular mass of 113 kDa.
2c) derived from standard proteins was somewhat larger for $LbADH^{K71E}$ (113 kDa) than for $LbADH^{WT}$ (95 kDa).
39$ mL between $LbADH^{WT}$ and $LbADH^{K71E}$ was consistent for three independent protein preparations with varying sample order and indicated a structural change triggered by the K71E exchange.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Structural change observed, but thermal stability unknown.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Thermal shift assay will reveal differences in melting temperature.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Perform thermal shift assay with SYPRO orange dye on purified proteins.
evolution_trajectory.observation: LbADH^{K71E} appeared to be slightly more stable with a melting point of 52.8°C compared to 51.5°C for wild-type.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Kinetic parameters for the main substrate should be determined.

evidence.quote_or_span: To test whether the K71E exchange has an influence on LbADH stability, the apparent melting point of the two protein variants was determined using a thermal shift assay with the dye SYPRO orange.
$LbADH^{K71E}$ appeared to be slightly more stable with a melting point of $52.
These predictions were supported by thermal shift assays revealing a slightly higher melting temperature for $LbADH^{K71E}$ than for $LbADH^{WT}$ .

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Stability slightly improved, but catalytic efficiency for 2,5-hexanedione not quantified.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How does K71E affect KM and Vmax for 2,5-hexanedione?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Kinetic analysis will show improved KM and Vmax compared to wild-type.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure NADPH oxidation at 340 nm with varying 2,5-hexanedione concentrations.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Kinetic analysis of the purified enzyme revealed that LbADH^{K71E} had a ~16% lowered KM value (4.3 mM) and a 17% higher Vmax (173.3 μmol/min/mg) for 2,5-hexanedione compared to the wild-type LbADH.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Test if improved kinetics extend to other substrates.

evidence.quote_or_span: Kinetic analysis of the purified enzyme revealed that $LbADH^{K71E}$ had a $\sim 16\%$ lowered $K_{M}$ value and a 17% higher $V_{max}$ for 2,5-hexanedione compared to the wild-type LbADH.
Consequently, $LbADH^{K71E}$ is more active than $LbADH^{WT}$ regarding the NADPH-dependent reduction of 2,5-hexanedione and has a higher affinity for this substrate.
The maximal activity calculated for $LbADH^{K71E}$ (173.

The kinetic properties of purified $LbADH^{WT}$ and $LbADH^{K71E}$ were analyzed using an assay in which the oxidation of NADPH was followed at 340 nm using a JASCO V560 UV/VIS spectrophotometer (JASCO, Gross-Umstadt, Germany) equipped with a water bath-heated cell holder set at 30 °C.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Improved kinetics for 2,5-hexanedione confirmed, but substrate scope unknown.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: Is the improvement specific to 2,5-hexanedione or general?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: K71E will show improved activity for other ketone substrates.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Measure activity of purified enzymes for methyl acetoacetate, 2-acetylpyridine, 2-hexanone, acetophenone, and 4-hydroxy-2-butanone.
evolution_trajectory.observation: LbADH^{K71E} showed 21–39% higher activity for 2-acetylpyridine, 2-hexanone, acetophenone, and 4-hydroxy-2-butanone. For methyl acetoacetate, K71E also showed better affinity and higher maximal activity than wild-type.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Structural basis for the improvement should be investigated.

evidence.quote_or_span: As summarized in Table 4, $LbADH^{K71E}$ showed 21–39% higher activity for 2-acetylpyridine, 2-hexanone, acetophenone, and 4-hydroxy-2-butanone, suggesting that the positive effect of the K71E exchange on LbADH activity was independent of the substrate.
Table 4 Analysis of $LbADH^{WT}$ and $LbADH^{K71E}$ activity for the substrates methyl acetoacetate, 2-acetylpyridine, 4-hydroxy-2-butanone, acetophenone, and 2-hexanone
Also for this substrate, $LbADH^{K71E}$ showed a better affinity and a higher maximal activity than $LbADH^{WT}$ (Fig.
To test whether the improved kinetic properties of $LbADH^{K71E}$ are specific for 2,5-hexanedione, we determined the $K_{M}$ and $V_{max}$ values for methyl acetoacetate.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: K71E improves activity for multiple substrates, but structural rationale missing.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: How does a surface mutation far from the active site improve catalysis?
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: Structural modeling will show that K71 is distant from the active site, suggesting an indirect mechanism.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Analyze the position of K71 in the LbADH crystal structure (PDB 1ZK4) using PyMOL.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Position 71 is not located close to the active center at the protein core, but solvent-exposed on the protein surface. The distance of position 71 to the substrate acetophenone is around 28.0 Å and to the cofactor NADPH approximately 14.1 Å.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: The distant location highlights a limitation in mechanistic understanding.

evidence.quote_or_span: position 71 is not located close to the active center at the protein core, but solvent-exposed on the protein surface
the distance of position 71 to the substrate acetophenone is around 28.0 Å and to the cofactor NADPH approximately 14.1 Å
The images were generated with the software PyMOL 2.2.0 (PDB code 1ZK4)

As summarized in Table 4, $LbADH^{K71E}$ showed 21–39% higher activity for 2-acetylpyridine, 2-hexanone, acetophenone, and 4-hydroxy-2-butanone, suggesting that the positive effect of the K71E exchange on LbADH activity was independent of the substrate.

evolution_trajectory.state_before: Structural analysis shows K71E is distant from active site, mechanism unclear.
evolution_trajectory.gap_or_uncertainty: The mechanism by which K71E improves activity remains unknown; only one variant characterized.
evolution_trajectory.hypothesis: The improvement may involve stabilization of a charged transition state or altered dynamics, but this is speculative.
evolution_trajectory.decision: Acknowledge limitations and propose future work.
evolution_trajectory.observation: Although it is still unclear how the K71E exchange positively influences the KM and vmax values, the improved catalytic and stability properties of the LbADH^{K71E} variant should be favorable for biotechnological applications. It is surprising that a single mutation on the protein surface is sufficient to increase enzyme activity. The stringent selection may have limited diversity, as only one variant was found.
evolution_trajectory.next_step_reason: Case complete; limitations noted for future studies.

evidence.quote_or_span: the amino acid at position 71 is not directly involved in substrate binding and reduction

Although it is still unclear how the K71E exchange positively influences the $K_{M}$ and $v_{max}$ values, the improved catalytic and stability properties the $LbADH^{K71E}$ variant should be favorable for biotechnological applications.
It is surprising that a single mutation on the protein surface, as is the case of the $LbADH^{K71E}$ variant, is sufficient to increase enzyme activity.

validation_methods: BioLector cultivation with and without substrate
Re-cloning of K71E into fresh pSenSox
Purification and biophysical characterization (SEC, SDS-PAGE, DLS)
metrics: Activity relative to wild-type for various substrates
success_verification.final_conclusion: The FACS-based screening using the pSenSox biosensor successfully identified the LbADH^{K71E} variant with improved catalytic properties for 2,5-hexanedione reduction and broader substrate scope. The mutation is a single charge reversal on the protein surface, distant from the active site, yet it enhances activity and slightly increases stability. The mechanism remains unclear.
limitations: Mechanism of K71E improvement not fully elucidated
Only one variant characterized; stringent selection may have limited diversity
Structural change observed but not fully explained
In vivo biosensor signal may not perfectly correlate with in vitro kinetics
